items.title: Hardwood Promises High-Speed JVM Apache Parquet Processing with Zero Mandatory Dependencies
items.summaryShort: Hardwood Promises High-Speed JVM Apache Parquet Processing with Zero Mandatory Dependencies. Hardwood, the project Gunnar Morling kick-started handling of Parquet files in Java, reached version 1.

items.title: OpenTelemetry Graduates to CNCF's Highest Maturity Level
items.summaryShort: OpenTelemetry Graduates to CNCF's Highest Maturity Level. The Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) has announced the graduation of OpenTelemetry, elevating the project to the foundation's highest level of maturity and formally recognizi

items.title: Mini book: Agentic AI Architecture
items.summaryShort: Mini book: Agentic AI Architecture. In this eMag, we try to establish agentic AI architecture as a new type of software architecture that will likely dominate the industry for years to come.

items.title: Oracle Quietly Halves Free Tier Ampere A1 Compute Limits with No Public Announcement
items.summaryShort: Oracle Quietly Halves Free Tier Ampere A1 Compute Limits with No Public Announcement. Oracle halved the Always Free Ampere A1 compute allowance from 4 OCPUs and 24 GB RAM to 2 OCPUs and 12 GB RAM with no public announcement.

items.title: Presentation: Fine Tuning the Enterprise: Reinforcement Learning in Practice
items.summaryShort: Presentation: Fine Tuning the Enterprise: Reinforcement Learning in Practice. The speakers discuss Agent RFT, OpenAI’s platform for fine-tuning reasoning models via real-time tool interactions and custom reward signals.

items.title: Politician who investigated spyware abuses had his phone hacked with Pegasus spyware

items.title: Last chance to apply — Startup Battlefield Australia applications close July 6
items.summaryShort: Last chance to apply — Startup Battlefield Australia applications close July 6. If you're going to apply for Startup Battlefield Australia, now is the time.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven't progressed as quickly as he'd hoped
items.summaryShort: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven't progressed as quickly as he'd hoped. At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.
